Source / Reference: Fleet Pulse: Status Column Spec — CHARGE↓ badge threshold
Conflict Description: Spec lists CHARGE score thresholds at <80% (4 pts) / <90% (7 pts) but the Status Column Spec describes the CHARGE↓ badge as firing when 'absorption is low' without a numeric cut-off.
Question to Client: Does the CHARGE↓ badge fire at absorption <90% (matching the 7-pt tier) or only <80% (matching the 0-pt tier)?

Source / Reference: Battery Health & Replacement Forecast — §3.5 CHARGE Signal
Conflict Description: The 4-pt CHARGE tier requires only absorption ≥80% — the short-trip fraction is not constrained at this tier. Unclear whether a vehicle with abs=82% and short=85% should land in 4 pts or 0 pts.
Question to Client: At the 4-pt CHARGE tier, is the short-trip fraction ignored (any value allowed) or does an implicit short<70% rule carry forward from the 7-pt tier?

Source / Reference: Battery Health & Replacement Forecast — §4 Forecast Logic
Conflict Description: Need a vehicle in staging whose modifier stack drives the forecast below 14 days so the floor + Critical override is testable.
Question to Client: Can the test team be provided with a sandbox vehicle/seeded data where (slope, modifiers) produce a base forecast <14 d before clamp?

Source / Reference: Fleet Pulse: Health Score Tooltip Spec — Sub-signal dot colours
Conflict Description: Spec states amber covers 40–79% and red covers <40%. The boundary value 40% itself is undefined — does a row at exactly 40% render amber or red?
Question to Client: Is the 40% boundary inclusive of amber (≥40% = amber) or red (<=40% = red)?

Source / Reference: Fleet Pulse: Status Column Spec — Column header
Conflict Description: Three candidate names appear across the design files: 'Status', 'Active Alerts', 'Health Flags'.
Question to Client: Which header text is final for the renamed column?

Source / Reference: Fleet Pulse: Status Column Spec — '+N more' overflow
Conflict Description: Unclear whether '+N more' is a hover-only tooltip, a click-to-expand chip, or both.
Question to Client: Is the '+N more' overflow interaction hover-only, click-only, or both (hover + click)?

Source / Reference: Fleet Pulse: Status Column Spec — EV vehicles
Conflict Description: Charge behaviour for BEVs is fundamentally different (regen / DC fast charge). Spec doesn't say if CHARGE↓ should be suppressed for EV stock (e.g. SEAL 627KB5).
Question to Client: Should the CHARGE↓ badge be suppressed on EV vehicles until EV-specific charge logic ships?
